mission.title: kpt: homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur)
mission.description: homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur). This issue affects 22+ users.

steps.title: Identify kpt troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kpt deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt -n kpt --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kpt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kpt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt
kubectl get configmap -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kpt
```
I'm running the beta version of macOS 11, and have been running into issue trying to `brew upgrade` to the latest version.

steps.title: Apply the fix for homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur)
steps.description:
> Another solution:
> 
> ```
> sudo rm -rf /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools
> sudo xcode-select --install
> ```
> 
> Thanks `brew doctor` for this hint.

I had tried that.
I would imagine it depends on the version of Xcode installed, I didn't have success with 12.2 but I did with the 12.3
```yaml
==> Upgrading 1 outdated package:
googlecontainertools/kpt/kpt 0.32.0 -> 0.33.0
==> Upgrading googlecontainertools/kpt/kpt 0.32.0 -> 0.33.0
==> Downloading https://github.com/GoogleContainerTools/kpt/archive/v0.33.0.tar.
Already downloaded: /Users/xander/Library/Caches/Homebrew/downloads/ac1b2a55ace325dc4b88fa6472234f6240ba15cd90ad89237df2812f6c8f5011--kpt-0.33.0.tar.gz
Error: Your CLT does not support macOS 11.0.
It is either outdated or was modified.
Please update your CLT or delete it if no updates are available.
```

steps.title: Upgrade kpt to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kpt:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kpt kpt/kpt --namespace kpt
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kpt
helm list -n kpt
```

steps.title: Confirm homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur) is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt -n kpt --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kpt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
> Another solution:
> 
> ```
> sudo rm -rf /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools
> sudo xcode-select --install
> ```
> 
> Thanks `brew doctor` for this hint.

I had tried that.
I would imagine it depends on the version of Xcode installed, I didn't have success with 12.2 but I did with the 12.3 CLT.

Not arguing, just noting the solution may not work for all.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kpt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
